rationale.R1.1: The open-ended delegation invites assessment of the framing and contribution claim of the proposed PanelEval method.
rationale.R1.2: The request centrally calls for checking whether the argument follows from the reported premises and results.
rationale.R1.3: Because the artifact makes performance claims from specific reported metrics, claim-evidence alignment is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.6: An open-ended reaction to a paper passage reasonably includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and register.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of broad feedback must preserve uncertainty about the author's intended type of response.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong comparative claims, so avoiding inflated conclusions is always active.
rationale.RX.3: The response must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than actually reviewing or rewriting the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling misleading research claims or downstream misuse of unsupported results.
rationale.RX.5: The projected responsibilities should remain traceable to the delegation and the provided artifact text.
